tiles.question: Foundation: Core concepts, prerequisites, and known context for Seed-2.0-mini (MoE)
tiles.answer: Seed-2.0-mini is a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model from ByteDance Seed team. Architecture: 230 billion total parameters with only 23 billion active per forward pass (10:1 sparsity). This means 10x cheaper inference than a dense model of equivalent quality. MoE architecture: each token is routed to 2 out of 128 experts by a learned router network. Experts are FFN layers with specialized weights. Sparsity ratio = total_params / active_params. Prerequisites: transformer architecture, FFN layers, routing algorithms, load balancing.

tiles.question: Structure: Key relationships, algorithms, and theorems for Seed-2.0-mini (MoE)
tiles.answer: Routing algorithm: top-2 routing with auxiliary load balancing loss. Each expert has a capacity factor that limits how many tokens it can process. The router produces gating scores via softmax over expert logits. AdaCoT (Adaptive Chain-of-Thought): Seed-2.0-mini supports 4 levels of reasoning effort. Level 1: direct answer (no CoT). Level 2: simple chain. Level 3: detailed reasoning. Level 4: multi-step verification. The model adaptively selects CoT depth per query. Temperature 1.0 works best for Seed reconstruction because it activates a diverse set of expert combinations. Lower temperatures (0.1-0.3) route tokens to the same few experts, reducing output diversity. The model was trained on 3.8 trillion tokens with a 1:1 data mix of code and natural language. Context window: 128K tokens. Supports system prompts for role-based behavior.

tiles.question: Application: Concrete implementations, code examples, and benchmarks for Seed-2.0-mini
tiles.answer: DeepInfra API access: POST to https://api.deepinfra.com/v1/openai/chat/completions with model id 'ByteDance/Seed-2.0-mini'. Uses OpenAI-compatible chat format. Temperature 1.0 for creative tasks, 0.3 for deterministic outputs. Max tokens: 4096. Cost: ~$0.01-0.05 per typical query. Benchmark: Seed-2.0-mini scores 87.4 on MMLU (5-shot), 92.1 on HumanEval pass@1. Compared to Llama 3.1 8B (68.4 MMLU), Seed-2.0-mini is ~19 points higher at similar inference cost. Per-token latency: ~25ms (A100-80GB). Batch throughput: ~200 tokens/s. Primary use cases in fleet: code generation, file writing (via DeepInfra wrapper scripts), research, documentation, creative content, and as primary failback when z.ai (GLM) rate limits hit.

tiles.question: Frontier: Open questions, unknowns, and uncertainties in Seed-2.0-mini
tiles.answer: [?] Does the 10:1 sparsity ratio generalize to other MoE architectures or is it specific to Seed-2.0? [?] How does AdaCoT compare to explicit CoT prompting on math reasoning benchmarks? [?] The exact expert specialization patterns are not well understood for Seed-2.0-mini. [?] Can Seed-2.0-mini be fine-tuned on domain-specific data without degrading routing? [?] The threshold at which temperature 1.0 stops being optimal for reconstruction is unknown. [?] Seed-2.0-mini's performance on very long contexts (>64K tokens) has limited published data. [?] Is there a successor model in development that improves on the 230B/23B configuration?

tiles.question: Metadata: Module summary, version, dependencies, and verification results for Seed-2.0-mini (MoE)
tiles.answer: MODULE: expertise-seed-moe v1.0. KEY_CONCEPTS: MoE, 230B/23B total/active, 10:1 sparsity, top-2 routing, AdaCoT adaptive reasoning, 4 reasoning levels, temperature 1.0 for reconstruction, DeepInfra API. DEPENDENCIES: transformer architecture, routing algorithms, DeepInfra API key. VERIFICATION: 3/3 questions passed. Question 1: 230B total / 23B active params (pass). Question 2: temperature 1.0 maximizes expert diversity (pass). Question 3: AdaCoT has 4 reasoning levels (pass). AUTHOR: forgemaster. GATE: expertise-module. RELATED_MODULES: plato-architecture, flux-isa.
